chapter: D. NEURONAL MIGRATION ANOMALY due to infection, ischemia, metabolic disorders

differentials.name: Atherosclerosis at carotid bifurcation =  upstream flow limitation WITHOUT significant intracranial collateral blood supply
differentials.raw: Atherosclerosis at carotid bifurcation =  upstream flow limitation WITHOUT significant intracranial collateral blood supply

differentials.name: Evolving ischemic condition / chronic infarct =  delayed reperfusion + vascular collateralization of incomplete acute / chronic infarct
differentials.raw: Evolving ischemic condition / chronic infarct =  delayed reperfusion + vascular collateralization of incomplete acute / chronic infarct

differentials.name: Vascular dysregulation =  hyperemia on symptomatic side with apparent perfusion abnormality on contralateral normal side
differentials.raw: Vascular dysregulation =  hyperemia on symptomatic side with apparent perfusion abnormality on contralateral normal side

differentials.name: Variations in cerebrovascular anatomy =  physiologic perfusion delay in circle of Willis variants, unequal blood supply between anterior + posterior circulation, congenitally absent ICA, etc.
differentials.raw: Variations in cerebrovascular anatomy =  physiologic perfusion delay in circle of Willis variants, unequal blood supply between anterior + posterior circulation, congenitally absent ICA, etc.

context: False Penumbra on Perfusion CT True penumbra:   successfully treatable with thrombolysis √  mean transit time (MTT) ↑ √  cerebral blood flow (CBF) ↓ √  cerebral blood volume (CBV) ↔ False penumbra:   area of abnormal perfusion as in ischemic penumbra NOT treatable with thrombolysis √  carotid bulb disease by CT angiography √  hypoattenuation in the same region √  restricted diffusion on DWI + ADC map Cause: √  perfusion abnormality ← vasospasm √  hyperintensity on STIR sequence √  no restricted diffusion √  no restricted diffusion
